Hi Ana, I am Tom. I just joined the QA team. Can you walk me through a normal day here?
Sure, Tom. Every morning, we start with a stand-up. Then I check the backlog and I prioritize the bugs.
Nice. And what are you working on right now?
Right now, I am testing the new payment feature, and I am tracking two open bugs.
How do you organize everything? Do you work alone?
No, I collaborate with developers in three countries. We work in two-week sprints and we track the progress on one board.
That is clear. Thanks. So who assigns the tickets?
The lead assigns each ticket. If I am busy, I give an estimate and we plan together.
Every morning, I check the backlog and prioritize the bugs.
Right now, I am testing the payment feature.
We work in two-week sprints and track everything on one board.
I collaborate with developers in three countries.
Could you give me an update, please?

Hi, and welcome to the QA team. Let me explain how a normal day works here. Every morning, we start with a fifteen-minute stand-up. Each tester gives a short update: what they finished yesterday, what they are working on today, and any blocker. After that, I open the backlog and I prioritize the bugs. The critical ones always come first. Then I assign each ticket to one tester, and we track the progress on the same board all day. We work in two-week sprints, so we plan on Monday and we review on Friday. One thing matters most here: when you find a critical bug, you report it right away. Clear communication keeps the whole team moving.

Hi Ana, it is Sofia. A quick update on where we are right now. At the moment, I am testing the new login feature, and it is looking good so far. The developers are fixing two bugs from yesterday, so I am waiting for the new build before I continue. Meanwhile, I am writing the test cases for the payment screen. The client is joining our call at three today, so I am preparing a short summary of our progress. I am not blocked, but I am a little behind, so I might need your help this afternoon. Talk soon.

First, Ana joins the fifteen-minute morning stand-up and gives her update. Then she opens the backlog and prioritizes the critical bugs. Next, the lead assigns each ticket to one tester. After that, Ana tests the feature she is handling today and tracks the progress. Finally, at the end of the day, the team reviews what they have finished.
